claudeMd:
# Project Context

You are working on Ring - Battle Royale, a top-down multiplayer stick-figure arena game.

## Task

Add a dialog with the controls — a dismissible overlay showing WASD movement, mouse aim, and click-to-shoot instructions.

## Tech Stack
- Node.js with `ws@^8.16.0` for WebSocket
- Vanilla JS client with HTML5 Canvas
- Custom test harness (not a framework)
- Dockerfile with node:20-alpine

## Conventions
- CommonJS modules (`require`/`module.exports`)
- `'use strict'` at top of each JS file
- Constants in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE at top of game.js
- Server-authoritative game state, client is rendering-only

## Commands
- Test: `npm test` (runs `node test/game.test.js`)
- Start: `npm start` (runs `node server/index.js` on port 8080)
- Build: `docker build .`

checks: The game loads in a browser showing a controls dialog overlay on first load
The controls dialog shows WASD for movement, Mouse for aim, Left Click for shoot
The dialog can be dismissed by clicking the button, clicking the backdrop, pressing Escape, or pressing H
After dismissal, pressing H re-opens the controls dialog
WebSocket connection initializes without being blocked by the dialog
All 44 existing unit tests pass when running npm test
